23921:
Diobel

Diobel is one of the larger towns under the rule of the city-state of Absalom. While not as large as Escadar off the Isle of Kortos' northern coast, Diobel plays an important role in the commercial and political affairs of the City at the Center of the World.
22769641:
Taggun Hold

The Chelaxian settlement of Taggun Hold is located in the Menador Mountains near the Nidalese border.
33703591:
Ice Spire

Ice Spire is a vast dungeon located in the northern plains of the Thanelands in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.
41453891:
Beneath the Docks

Beneath the Docks is a neighborhood beneath the wooden planks of the district, inhabited by the area's poorest residents, those with criminal connections, or those who prefer to live as close to the water as possible. The Seafoam Conclave, comprised of azarketi, is the largest community here, though they are generally poorer than those living in Gilltown, often working as garbage collectors or fishermen. Jossie Slimfin is one such garbage collector, selling items to surface dwellers as aquatic treasures and also acting as an informant between the merfolk and cecaelia communities living deeper underground. The communities here tend to be insular, with little interaction with the outside world, though in 4711 AR, many cecaelia arrived from Xidao in pursuit of a criminal who had already been killed by the Pathfinder Society, and many stayed to live here.
45246921:
Nirfan

Nirfan is a Qadiran city in the south of the Maharev region, which is one of the safest and most fertile regions in all Qadira. Nirfan itself sits on the eastern bank of the Meraz River a few miles upriver of the town of Isa; the Maharev Jungle lies along the opposite bank.
50765501:
Ashen Bulwark

The Ashen Bulwark is an abandoned dwarven fortress.
51098951:
Mephit Spring

Mephit Spring is a valley in the Footprints of Rovagug mountains of western Osirion that is the locus of several powerful elemental forces. Located between the Kho-Rarne Pass and Tar Kuata, the springs once were the home of many scamps who communed peacefully with one another. As of 4714 AR the mood has darkened, as a demon had taken over the area.
51548411:
Brimstone Falls

The Brimstone Falls is a 60-foot-tall waterfall on the Kurka River in the Mwangi Expanse that flows into a wide lagoon at the edge of the Kurka Swamp. The many small channels that form the cataract throw up enough spray that a permanent rainbow hangs above it. The slope of the waterfall's cliff is not steep, and numerous switchback trails climb up its face.
60721151:
Baifen

Baifen serves as a central gathering place for the various small Tapestry People communities within the lands of the Szaezan Crags leased by Hao Jin. The day-to-day running of the town is done collectively by about a hundred families.

Baifen is a popular holiday destination because of its many attractions, such as public theater performances, hot spring bathhouses, and night markets filled with delicious foods. Historians and adventurers are also attracted to the area, with the hope that one of the Tapestry People can help solve ancient riddles or provide otherwise lost historical knowledge.
63446201:
Belde

Belde is a town in western Cheliax situated on the banks on the Maiestas River, between the Menador Mountains and the Barrowood. Despite being geographically located in the Archduchy of the Heartlands, it serves as capital of the rebellious Archduchy of Hellcoast.
65932781:
Little Inner Sea

Little Inner Sea is a neighborhood in The Puddles where members of the Salvage Guild meet in a completely flooded area. It was once a public park, but the saltwater hasn't been kind to the trees. Although scavenging is one of the few legal professions in the district, the taxes levied by Nomarch Haigen Topkick mean that little profit can be made. Those who can't afford it have to go out at night, with the inherent dangers, as strange creatures fill the lake and the wounds common in this type of work easily become infected. The shops around the lake are among the best-maintained and busiest in the district.
70513631:
Irrere

The exclusively gnomish town of Irrere, located on the island of Irrere in the Ironbound Archipelago, is renowned for its exotic architecture. The establishment of the gnome monastery of Shay Citadels close to the town led to an increase in the town's population.

Its residents enforce a policy of extreme isolationism, although gnomes frequently make pilgrimages to Irrere, especially those who are feeling the effects of the Bleaching. The monks of the Shay Citadels research this ancestral curse and seek ways to resist its effects. The town is also welcoming to duskwalker gnomes.

Although few natives leave Irrere, those who do often possess great wisdom and insight—a result of deep self-reflection typically uncharacteristic of other gnomes.
92280031:
Blackpipe

Blackpipe is a small farming town in Numeria, named after the large adamantine pipe that sticks out of the ground in the farming area's fertile soil.
97144021:
Thornkeep

The small town of Thornkeep is located within the Echo Wood of the northwestern River Kingdoms, about 10 miles east of the West Sellen River and 100 miles northwest of Daggermark at the junction of the Mosswater, Dagger, and Forest roads. Its reputation as a rowdy and dangerous settlement draws unscrupulous adventurers, bandits, and entrepreneurs for many types of illicit trade. The large number of nearby ruins and monsters attracts many adventurers looking to start making a name for themselves. This includes those interested in the nearby Emerald Spire.

Thornkeep has a wizard's guild called the Goldenfire Order.
105428571:
Elokolobha

The city of Elokolobha is carved out of a massive limestone formation on the eastern edge of the Screaming Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse. It is inhabited by fey bilokos and elokos.
145816531:
Frothripper

Frothripper is a waterfall about 200 feet tall that is fed from the Kortos Mounts north of Absalom.

The waterfall hides an abandoned bardic college once known as Allegro College.
152180901:
Azari Palace

The Azari Palace is an ossified remnant of the period when worship of Aroden was a prominent feature of life in Absalom, before his death and the dawn of the Age of Lost Omens. Aroden's church was once an integral part of civic life in the City at the Centre of the World, but, with his passing in 4606 AR, his followers fell to infighting and despair. Many worshippers converted to other faiths, but 5,000 years of history, relics, and influence do not disperse quite as easily. The Azari Palace is the physical embodiment of this. Once called Saint Solian Temple, it once served as the second-largest temple of Aroden in the Ascendant Court; the largest ex-cathedral in the district now serves as the Chelaxian embassy. Nevertheless, it is still one of the district's most beautiful buildings.

Azari Palace is named after its owners, House Azari, one of Absalom's numerous noble houses that traces its roots back to one of Aroden's earliest disciples and was intrinsically linked to Aroden's church. Dozens of House Azari's scions were elected to lead the church of Aroden, and three members of the house, including St. Solian, ascended to become saints of Aroden's religion. House Azari's support of the crumbling church of Aroden cost them everything, bankrupting the family and forcing them to sell their palatial estate in the Petal District. Led by house patriarch Lord Toiden, they moved into St. Solian Temple instead.

As more and more churches and temples of Aroden have closed, the Azari Palace has become a vast repository for relics and artifacts of the now-dead religion. With no new worshippers coming to the faith and House Azari fallen in all but name, the Azari Palace has a feeling of forlorn abandonment, with near-endless chambers of dusty relics and forlorn treasures left forgotten in a temple to a god that Golarion has moved on from.
159646081:
Qadlus Mavari

Qadlus Mavari is a Qadiran city located in the Northern Zho Mountains area, and is the north-easternmost settlement in Qadira. Qadlus Mavari is a flourishing oasis town built around a shrine dedicated to Lugalisimaru, the so called "Palm Tree King". The town does a healthy trade in selling offerings that can be left at other shrines to Lugalisimaru that are built at each of the oases that dot the desert beyond. Qadlus Mavari also boasts a booming trade in water-producing magic items.
162444981:
Stone of the Seers

Magnimar's most respectable school of arcane instruction, the Stone of the Seers, stands next to the Seerspring Gardens and its legendary spring.

Kintargo Opera House

176377641:
Lady's Light

The Lady's Light is an enormous statue located on the western end of Lady's Cape, in the south-westernmost reaches of Varisia's Mushfens approximately 50 miles from the city of Magnimar as the crow flies. Almost 200 feet in height, the statue resembles a woman in flowing robes with one breast bared, holding a staff aloft.
189016751:
Nerosyan

The capital of the former crusader state of Mendev, Nerosyan was designed first and foremost as a defensive fortification to support the Mendevian Crusades and hold back the endless demonic hordes that once emerged from the since-closed Worldwound.
191699211:
Zharech

Zharech is a town in western Mendev known for its gladiatorial combat sports.
192255291:
Frozen Pines

The Frozen Pines is a nightmare glade in the Grungir Forest that even the forest's fey fear to visit. This frozen region consists of several dozen square miles of territory.

Its pine trees have adapted to the frost, but nothing else grows here, including any of the forest's normal undergrowth. Frozen Pines is said to be under the control of the White Witches of Irrisen, but in truth it serves as the hunting grounds for powerful wendigos.
198478061:
Changdo

Changdo (pronounced CHANG-dew), also called the Imperial City, is the capital of Po Li and was the original capital of Lung Wa, the empire that dominated Tian Xia until the death of Aroden.
214794231:
Crescent Manor

The Crescent Manor was the residence of the information broker Grandmaster Torch, who left the city and vanished into the shadows to some unknown location. Sometimes the lights are seen on upstairs, fueling rumors of his imminent return.
215163441:
Yelligo Wharf

Yelligo Wharf is a small pirate settlement on the island of Dahak's Tooth in the Shackles.

The settlement was established in 4709 AR and hides itself in Leatherwing Bay. The ramshackle settlement consists of a few squat buildings made of driftwood and dirt clinging to the shore. It is home to four pirate crews that prey on the merchant ships travelling close to the mainland, east of Shenchu Bay. Kamshika Heavyhand captains the armored sloop Baleful Glare, Mean Steebyn captains the barkentine Rough Customer, Noila the Knife captains the brig Harm's Way, and Pendahl of Rahadoum captains the sloop-of-war Baalzebul.

These four captains capture Inner Sea merchant vessels on their way home from Port Peril, stealing their cargoes and selling their crews to the cultists of Dahak for sacrifice. Chadric of Laekastel, who lives in the only stone building of the town, is the apparent head of the business.
219767651:
Katapesh

2 locations:

  • Jexler & Young Salvage Company and Deep Sea Exploration and Retrieval
226050701:
Taal Mornat

Approximately six centuries ago, dwarves constructed the ossuary of Taal Mornat especially for the servants of the Cathedral of Magrim in Larrad as well as other faithful servants of the dwarven god. The necropolis lies some twenty miles west of the city in a hidden valley within the peaks of the Five Kings Mountains. Over time, due to the fame of many laid to rest there, the site is now a holy and cultural centre for the dwarven community.
230810471:
Avenue of the Gods

The Avenue of the Gods is a wide road studded with over a dozen temples and shrines that runs through Almas' East Hill neighborhood. Its spectacular architecture attracts the pious and curious alike who come to visit and admire the former Cathedral of Aroden (now a Pathfinder Society lodge), the Golden Cathedral, the temples of Abadar, Cayden Cailean, Erastil, Pharasma, Shelyn, and Torag, along with smaller chapels to various empyreal lords and other demigods.
231513371:
Gojko

Gojko is a tiny Irriseni village located directly on the border with the Lands of the Linnorm Kings in the province of the Verge.
241864701:
Tern Hill

Tern Hill is a cavern in the Verduran Forest that collapsed, creating an open, roofless space. Tall trees surround the hill, their canopies blocking the view, while their thick roots are visible inside the cavern. Several tunnels connect the cavern's chambers, making it an easily defensible location.

The Rootweft Lodge used the cavern as a base of operations during the Wildwood Revolt, taking advantage of its defensive strengths.
244119881:
Zunderwal Hold

Zunderwal Hold is a long-fallen dwarven fortress in the Stained Peaks near Avistan's approach to the Crown of the World. It has long been occupied by bugbears, who fight an ongoing battle for supremacy over who should hold the title of "Huntmaster of the Hold".

Sojourner's Rest

Sojourner's Rest is a village in the southern Grungir Forest of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, situated in the foundations of Thornwall Castle.

281798691:
Mithril Chef

The Mithril Chef is a high-end restaurant that also serves as a second-mark Firebrands cell located in The Docks district. All its staff are members of the organization, led by Claudette Butterfoot, who is more focused on cooking than going on adventures, though she frequently gives her employees "vacations", after which news of their deeds is heard around the city. The food they serve is excellent, very varied, and with strong aromas that make a good impression and one can also see the chefs using juggling techniques to prepare it, which always entertains the customers. They have a rivalry with the Bladed Bastards, although their fights are staged as a distraction until agents from both groups collaborate to free prisoners and help the population.
291632071:
Anglemire

Anglemire was a coastal town in Cheliax and stronghold of House Davian until their defeat during the Chelaxian Civil War. House Thrune, who had defeated House Davian's forces at the nearby city of Corentyn in the Battle of a Hundred Kings in 4639 AR, then starved House Davian's nobles to death in a year-long siege of the town's keep of Emberhold, torched the keep, and withdrew all support for the town. Its surviving residents persisted until orcs, trolls, and other dangers drove them away, leaving the town an abandoned ruin of stone structures.

Wanshi

Wanshi is the capital of Hogosareta Province in Minkai, as well as the primary commercial port and naval base on the nation's southern coastline.
316038021:
Solscrene

Solscrene is located on the stretch of the Sellen River within the Verduran Forest, and it faces Andoran on the river's opposite bank. As one travels north from Cassomir to the Isle of Arenway, Solscrene is considered to be the last village before one reaches the true deep forest. Solscrene sits on a small craggy island and its inhabitants are rather insular, constantly circulating foul stories about distant Galtans, neighbouring Andorens, the mysterious druids of Arenway and even gnomes.
316692111:
Erspurn

Little is known about Erspurn, a settlement of western Osirion during its First Age located in what is now Rahadoum.

331252501:
Mount Gustus

Mount Gustus is a mountain in the Five Kings Mountains located northeast of the Darkmoon Vale near Kykar. It is one of the tallest peaks in the area at an elevation of 14,806 feet, and the fearsome red dragon Daralathyxl lairs in the nearby foothills.
335796321:
Moon Silver Pool

The Moon Silver Pool is located at the northernmost edge of the Arthfell Forest. It is composed of highly concentrated silver water, and although the place is very beautiful, it is extremely dangerous for the pool is guarded by a cabal of druids and werewolves considers it sacred and attacks anyone outside their group who dares approach. Rumors exist that the pool was created by Narven, the druid king of the forest, though its purpose remains unknown.

343174691:
Trivardum

Trivardum was a meeting place for the three most powerful families of Westcrown: House Drovenge, House Julistarc, and House Oberigo. Today, it serves as a local market and inn. The suite was booked in 4716 AR by Vors Kyniar, the ambassador of Taldor to Westcrown.
344707501:
Quantium

Quantium, a port city on the Obari Ocean, is the capital of the nation of Nex. The city prides itself on its diversity; xenophobia is frowned upon. The city has many palaces, hanging gardens, and open-air mazes, as well as several reputable universities of arcane magic and lore. The city's magnificence has been compared with Oppara in Taldor and Sothis in Osirion.

There are many statues of the archmage Nex, who took control of the then-city-state in -987 AR, as well as of the heroes who helped him forge the kingdom that still bears his name.
370882431:
Ghasterhall

Ghasterhall is an infamous library located in Virlych. It is also known as the Palace of Travesties. Although a place of retained knowledge, Ghasterhall displays horrific methods of storing information. Ghasts possessing oracular power stalk the blood-splattered halls and dead victims are nailed to the walls, used for necromantic experimentation.

Ghasterhall has been described as an inverted tower-pit, serving as both prison and library. The awakened demilich Geir acts as head researcher for Tar-Baphon, establishing himself as curator of the massive library, laboratory, and prison. Although a horde of undying scholars still possess tremendous accumulated knowledge, only Geir continues with his endless experimentation on esoteric matters.

Ambition for dark knowledge waned with the imprisonment of the Whispering Tyrant, but after his escape in 4719 AR, Tar-Baphon sought Geir's aid in developing a method of either transforming the orcs of Belkzen into an undead army or destroying them.

For a time, Lucimar the Lich-Wolf sequestered himself in the prison-library. By 4711 AR, Ghasterhall served as headquarters for the Whispering Way.
381623981:
Vanderale Villa

Vanderale Villa is the home of Magnimar's House Vanderale, one of the city's oldest families.
384640861:
Flying Alderman

The Flying Alderman is a modest inn on the edge of the Foreign Quarter, serving as a safe house for Pathfinder Society agents. Drock Ovix, the innkeeper, is a Pathfinder himself and allows them to use tunnels beneath the inn that connect directly to the Grand Lodge—a closely guarded secret of the organization. Agents can spend three nights free at the inn, while others must pay two silver pieces, which includes bed, dinner, and breakfast the next day.
392069311:
Zharchovsk

Zharchovsk is a small town in southern Irrisen, located at the end of a mountain pass near the southeastern border of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.
394032851:
Kendall Amphitheater

The Kendall Amphitheater, located in the Heights District of Korvosa, hosts open-air performances.
408843901:
Vaneo Drovenge

Vaneo Drovenge was the three-story mansion of the powerful House Drovenge, which wielded considerable influence in the city. But in 4710 AR, the family collapsed, and thieves stole all the mansion's valuables.
417928511:
Administrative and Civic Center of Ravounel

447053601:
Heliu's Folly

Heliu's Folly was one of the westernmost fortresses of the defensive line known as the Sunwall in the Hold of Belkzen.
456235511:
Izzet

Izzet is a Qadiran city in the Meraz Desert region that separates Qadira from the rest of the Empire of Kelesh. The Meraz Desert once used to be fertile land before it was sapped of life by the Heralds of Dust. Izzet is located along the coastline where the desert meets the Obari Ocean, between the cities of Ihalar to the north and Heger to the south.
477328101:
Duwwor

Duwwor is the fourth-largest of Thuvia's five allied city-states. It is situated in the south of the country on the edge of the Barrier Wall mountains, near the headwaters of the Turvanar River. Its ruler is Kharane, Defender of Duwwor.
481941151:
Pyramid of Doom

The Pyramid of Doom is a site in southern Osirion. It is located to the west of the Salt Hills.
484605791:
Morozny

The city of Morozny is the regional capital of the Irriseni province of Wintercrux.
493318991:
Ardagh

Ardagh is the northernmost settlement of the Ustalavic county of Odranto, located on the Moutray River, hard against the unforgiving lands of the Worldwound.
507798701:
Katapesh

Sprawling across a valley of blazing-hot sand, is one of the most incredible and wondrous cities in all of Golarion: the great desert metropolis of Katapesh (pronounced KAT-a-pesh), the City of Trade. Katapesh is a city of organized chaos where cash is king and commerce is the highest god.

From its early beginnings as the Golden City, Katapesh was rich in natural resources and is located on the shores of the Obari Ocean. It retained its mercantile importance no matter who occupied the city.

The metropolis of Katapesh is the home to many bazaars and fairs that sell anything one might desire. Two notable permanent markets within the city are the Peculiar Emporium and the Nightstalls.

Cathedral of Abadar

Magnimar's Cathedral of Abadar is the largest temple in the city and is led by Jyronn Imikar. Located in the Naos district, this temple also serves as the main bank and moneylender for the city and surrounding areas of Varisia. Several dozen followers of Abadar live here, ranging from lay personnel to clerics and paladins. The church helps patrol the city and sends agents into the field to keep abreast of happening in the larger region. Few know that the church also maintains a prison in the Osprey piling of the Irespan. This prison is for political prisoners and those the city and church wish to disappear.
566128481:
Yemhasin

Yemhasin is a neighborhood in Absalom where the Yamasan diaspora goes about their daily lives. Most are farmers, and even if they don't sell their vegetables, they likely have a garden where they grow them for personal use. Initial opinions about them were negative because they couldn't use their agricultural skills in an urban setting, and compared to the Lirgeni diaspora, they didn't assimilate well to the city. However, over time, they adapted, using whatever space they had to plant crops and preserve their culinary culture, while also leaving behind the caste system of their original nation.

Alvis

Located in the Arthfell region of southeastern Andoran near Aspo Bay, the town of Alvis is best known for a series of kidnappings perpetrated by a local cult in 4708 AR. Alvis is also home to the Andoran Alchemical Society.

In 4708 AR the Sheriff of Alvis was an idealistic young man named Eraltis.

In 4725 AR many people came to the town to join the guild to investigate the effects of the Godsrain, creating a need for new housing.
608693081:
Arazlant Mox

Arazlant Mox is the highest peak in the Kortos Mounts, the mountain range that dominates the center of the Isle of Kortos. It is over 26,400 feet in height. The peak was the first home of the Starstone when Aroden raised the island from the sea, and is sacred to all four Ascended gods.

Dimayen

Dimayen is a sizable farming settlement located in southern Qadira in the Meraz Desert south of the Meraz River.

Dhoffram Keep

Dhoffram Keep is located in the center of the Chelaxian city of Corentyn. It was the former lord's residence, before the rise of the House Thrune. Now it is used only by the Church of Asmodeus. No one knows what goes on inside and the citizens of Corentyn usually avoid the Zelkie Park outside the keep.

The Dhoffram Keep is one of the nine famous forts of Corentyn, according to the classical listing.

Black Tarn

Black Tarn is an infamous mountain lake on the Ironbound Island of Dragon's Rib in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.
671582751:
Blood Rise Rock

Blood Rise Rock is a sacred Lamashtan stronghold in the foothills of the Zho Mountains, about 50 miles from Dimayen, that has been carved into the form of the Lamashtu's torso over centuries of occupation. It is said to contain a plague-bearing spring, and was once controlled by the Bone Splinter clan of kholo.

The Slace Block

1 locations:

684486031:
Eran's Rest

Eran's Rest is a remote but picturesque settlement within the Hungry Mountains, perched on the eastern side of the Ghorcha Pass among the Hundred Haunted Vales and overlooking the Adghain Valley. It is located in the Ustalavic county of Amaans, and a steep goat path provides access to the village.

The village is dominated by herders and weavers but is also associated with fairy tales and other stories of badly-behaved fey. The villagers portray these tales in their art and architecture as well as their stories. Unfortunately, there may be more to the tales than mere story as the village suffers regular disappearances and other strange events.
688773821:
Blackcove

Blackcove is a small and very isolated fishing village on Cheliax's western Hellmouth Gulf. The ruined island of Nal-Kashel is located two miles off the coast from the village.
694938521:
Jol

The city of Jol (pronounced JOL) is located to the south of the Grungir Forest and is the capital of Southmoor in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. The city is old and built on the ruins of the Thassilonian city of Torandey; it was founded in 1102 AR by King Eskir Honeytongue. As of 4719 AR, it is ruled by Linnorm King Ostog the Unslain, who recently came to power after his predecessor Opir Eightfingers vanished.
695400791:
Eranmas

The walled city of Eranmas is on the shores of Lake Encarthan. It is the second largest city in Molthune, and home of the country's navy. This navy is the largest fleet on the lake, and its main role is to blockade Tamran, capital of the "rebel" nation of Nirmathas. The city is governed by Lord Resket.
700035621:
Everforge

Everforge is a tomb dedicated to Naika Omenspeaker near the now-abandoned Sky Citadel of Kravenkus in the World's Edge Mountains of modern-day Taldor.
722642401:
Lethe Springs

Lethe Springs is a magical hot spring located high in the Southern Zho Moutnains of Qadira in sight of the mountains Zhonar and Zhobl. While the springs seem like the perfect place to relax and warm oneself from the chill mountain air, its waters carry a terrible curse: drinking the waters obliterates one's memories and transforms the body into a fiendish mockery of oneself.
727840001:
Cinnabar Market

Cinnabar Market is a famous marketplace of cinnabar in Lingshen that also unofficially serves as a liaison point for the region's criminal elements.
731471361:
Mallaru

Mallaru (pronounced mal-ya-ru) is the sole port city of the nation of Linvarre, which was previously the Taldan colony of Amanandar. The city is a recent expansion of the new nation's territory into lands formerly claimed by Shenmen and is located where the Ilushe River empties into the Sea of Ghosts.
736967841:
Gadbadigat

Gadbadigat is an outlaw city located at the heart of the arid Narhari Desert, one of the most rugged and harsh regions of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. The Bahig River, the desert's largest source of water, disappears into a miles-wide sinkhole around which Gadbadigat is built.
746340561:
Storm Tower

The Storm Tower is located in a valley in the Tusk Mountains with high hills surrounding it. The area around the valley can be used by any orc hold, and the tower offers a perfect view of the skies, so the Storm-Screamers of Rull often make pilgrimages to the structure from time to time.
751983571:
Kerrick

Kerrick is the largest town in the Swardlands, located west of the mouth of the Husna River on the western coast of the Isle of Kortos.
764028191:
Valas's Gift

Valas's Gift was a small town in the north of the nation of Mendev, located on the road north from Kenabres. Although it was populated as of 4711 AR, the town was nothing more than overgrown ruins by 4725 AR, and all that remained were bubbling springs of blood-red water said to be the fertilizing blessing of Valas, a holy man.
779757721:
Lepidstadt

Founded in 3898 AR, Lepidstadt is the largest settlement in Ustalav's county of Vieland and effectively acts as the county's capital since the governing council is based there. Lepidstadt is located on the banks of one of Ustalav's numerous rivers, west of the foothills of the enormous and near-impassible Tusk Mountains that mark the border with the Hold of Belkzen.
785762191:
Rastel

The ruined city of Rastel was once a thriving settlement of the now-extinct nation of Rastel. The ruins stand upon the banks of the Upper Korir River, in the Korir Plains in the Mwangi Expanse. In common with all ruins of the Rastel culture, the ruined city of Rastel shows signs of fire, conflict, and cannibalism.

Today, the ruins are home to a small number of cambions of Zenj descent. They carry out the traditional Zenj practice of ancestor worship, and include their fiendish ancestors within this. Among these are a group of fiends the cambions refer to as the Waiting Ones, who they believe are asleep beneath the city.
808276941:
Dravod Knock

The muddy fishing town of Dravod Knock is located on the eastern banks of Lake Prophyria in the Kellid nation of Numeria. Across the lake lies the city of Karcau, but Dravod Knock could not be more different from that cultured, Ustalavic city. Its houses are little more than crude wooden shacks speckled with generations of river silt, while its people make their simple living from fishing the waters of Lake Prophyria, or firing bricks using the copious mud found along its shore. Despite their poverty, the Kellid inhabitants of Dravod Knock are a proud bunch who cling to ancient traditions, many of which seem to revolve around the very mud that seems to be the town's foremost identifier. Countless social occasions are tied to the mud, from how they bury their dead to how they execute their criminals.
809699941:
Wave Door

The Wave Door is a dimensional storage space left as a hidden treasure in Cutlass Cove by the pathfinders of Delvehaven as an additional way to enter the complex by disabling its wards beforehand. It was only accessible at night by following the area's geography and using a wayfinder at a specific point, making it nearly impossible to find by chance.
829552951:
Troglodyte Caves

The Troglodyte Caves are located within the Lady's Cape and are named after the xulgath tribe that lives there, the Children of Zevgavizeb. The caverns connect to a tunnel system that provides access to the Lady's Light, though few besides the xulgath could use them due to the foul odor that permeates all the caves.
831434381:
House of Whips

The House of Whips is a shop catering to sadomasochists, run by a woman who wears red silks and is an open secret to be a succubus who drains the energy of her more healthy customers through the lashing of her whips. She uses magic to control the minds of her "employees" and sometimes struggles to resist acquiring a beautiful new toy that enters her establishment.
835253921:
Kyzuv

Kyzuv is the de facto capital of the Erutaki nation of Yumyzyl. Located at the mouth of the Moraghiin River, Kyzuv's central location makes it the site of the annual gathering of Yumyzyl clans, which multiplies its normally small population fivefold.
837457121:
Rolan's Shop

Rolan's Shop is a tinker shop that sells automatons and other engineering products, although in 4710 AR its owner went a little crazy with all the chaos in the city and went out into the streets to barricade himself around his shop and attack anyone who approached.
847954601:
Paddlefoot Farm

The Paddlefoot Farm is located near the Soggy River, and the halfling family that runs it doesn't make money from the farm itself, but rather from the illegal toilets they smuggle into the river and along the roads. Their illegal activities have been largely ignored because Sandpoint has more pressing problems to solve. The farm's appearance is terrible, and it's clear that no one here takes farm work seriously.
851949791:
Golquila

The walled city of Golquila is one of the numerous prosperous ports that rim the Betul Sea in the Western Ghats region of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. Its defences are enhanced by being built into the mountains. Further protection for the city is provided by two huge gorillas and their keepers who stay near the city. Golquila is located at one end of the Seabound Road, the completion of which heralded a new era of prosperity for the once-desolate region of the Western Ghats.
856788391:
Tephu

Tephu, the City of the Reed People is a city in the Sphinx Basin region of Osirion named for the reed-covered marshlands where the rivers Crook and Asp join. The city's major industry is its near-monopoly on the harvest and processing of the abundant papyrus. Consequently, the city houses the largest academy of scribes in Osirion, who have preserved a massive catalogue of records from the time of its founding in -1605 AR by Djederet II, pharaoh and cleric of Nethys. Tephu's current leader is the preservationist governor: Haty-a of Tephu Deka An-Keret.
861089381:
Veldraine

The town of Veldraine is perhaps the most important strategic holding of Korvosa, and the second-oldest Chelaxian settlement in Varisia.

Located at the mouth of Conqueror's Bay, Veldraine's ships make up most of the Korvosan Navy, and its naval academy trains most of the city-state's sailors and captains. It also contains a giant winch that allows the military to pull an immense chain taut across the entrance to the bay, keeping invading navies or pirates out of the waters—or trapped within it to be dispatched one by one. The chain is inspected monthly by Korvosan officials, and has only been used once in 4685 AR in order to fight pirates from Riddleport.
864219281:
City Hall

3 locations:
  • Bank of Abadar
  • Longacre Building
  • Whitecaps
879850561:
Feaster's Dint

Feaster's Dint is a geothermal energy zone near the Wolfrun Hills where a clan of 20 magmin resides. The skittish creatures are not usually a problem for explorers in the region, but it should be noted that they infrequently attack those who enter their territory, and the guards of the settlements in the area consider them a potential threat.
883902391:
Citadel Vraid

Citadel Vraid is the fortress-headquarters of the Hellknight Order of the Nail, indeed it is sometimes referenced as the Fortress of the Nail. It was constructed by the city of Korvosa in an effort to successfully entice the Order to move to Varisia. It is located about 50 miles southwest of Korvosa on a narrow plateau named after one of Korvosa's founders, Montlarion Jeggare, and is on the edge of the southern Mindspin Mountains.
895445601:
Ishad

Ishad is one of the numerous large, prosperous cities that dot the fertile Qadiran region of Pashman. It is located on the banks of the Pashman River along Pashman's south-western border with the neighbouring region of Tapur.
914647501:
Yan Ulvedro's Fort

Yan Ulvedro's Fort was built in the earliest days of the nation of Xopatl by the Arcadian hero-god Ulvedro to help defend the nation against its enemies. Located high in the Mildanesi Mountains, Yan Ulvedro's Fort was designed to protect Xopatl from the neighbouring Razatlan Empire and assisted on the few occasions when Xopatl was attacked. As the power of Razatlan waned, Yan Ulvedro's Fort gradually became more akin to a monastery, and pilgrims from both Xopatl and neighbouring Innazpa now visit the sages of Yan Ulvedro's Fort to seek wisdom and learn their unique mystical techniques. These sages can help travellers reach enlightenment by temporarily pulling their soul out of their body.
917182381:
Battlefield

This Battlefield is littered with the corpses of boggards and xulgaths from the Lady's Cape tribes, who clashed in a decisive battle where the boggards won, though they lost a third of their own. The smell of the area is horrific, and someone has taken all the heads of the xulgaths, whose decapitated bodies lie on the ground.
927157151:
Prophet's Home

1 locations:
  • Grand Grounds of the Splendid Statuary
929606111:
Erastil's Alehouse

Erastil's Alehouse is a popular establishment run by clergymen of Erastil on the edge of the Avenue of the Hopeful, so there is plenty of traffic to offer courage in the form of beer to those trying to become gods.
933218371:
Aulorian Estate

Aulorian Estate, located in The Greens district of Kintargo, Ravounel, is the home of House Aulorian.
951347291:
Siavenian

Siavenian is a small town in the northern Fierani Forest of Kyonin, just east of the Endowhar River. It grew up around a disused elf gate that was activated by a group of adventurers fleeing the Darklands in 4708 AR.

After the Godsrain the elf gate melted into a pile of strange metallic liquid that none have dare touch yet.
983854931:
Little Miss Drurie's

The Little Miss Drurie's is a dance hall located in the Jarvis End district of Kintargo. Dance halls in Kintargo combine the functions of a musical venue, tavern, and brothel. As of 4719 AR, it was one of Kintargo's most recently established dance halls and had garnered a following for its dance contests and experimental musical performances.
986775811:
Slipcove

Slipcove is a small port on Bag Island in the Shackles.

The lord of Bag Island, Jolis Raffles, attacks the slave ships of Cheliax and offers freed slaves the chance to live on Bag Island. As a result, Slipcove has the largest halfling population in the Shackles.
987820811:
Circle of Hierophants

The Circle of Hierophants is a circle of stone menhirs created in Old Sarkoris and located in the Stonewilds of modern Sarkoris. Druids of the Green Faith worshiped here, as this was the greatest of their stone circles.

Hundreds of stones of varied sizes make up the many circles located at the site. Many of the circles has stones placed at exact angles beyond the known ability of man to build repeatedly. Each stone has rituals, legends, and secrets carved into them. Unlocking these carvings is beyond those who do not practice the Green Faith. Some of these circles are on small hills overlooking the main circle, giving the area a feeling of depth. Six of the circles are around cave mouths that reportedly led to deeper caves and even more stone circles. While leaders of the faith once protected these caves, after the opening of the Worldwound corrupted siabraes lived within.
993683041:
Hazrak

Hazrak is a minotaur settlement located in the Riven Hills of the Isle of Kortos in Absalom. Its inhabitants can trace their town's origins all the way back to the First Siege of Absalom in 23 AR. During this war, the minotaur warlord Voradni Voon brought thousands of his kind through the Brazen Arch to lay siege to Absalom. After their defeat by the god Aroden himself, the surviving minotaurs fled to the central highlands of the island, where they (or their descendants) founded Hazrak, and continue to harass the other races of the island to this day.
1016665521:
Velecia Nora

Velecia Nora is an isolated snow-covered fort made of iron and stone in the Irriseni province known as the Verge. When a unit of soldiers demonstrates cowardice or disobedience, it is marched to Velecia Nora where it receives what is known as the Velecian Cruelty. One soldier in each group of ten is selected in random and beaten to the death by his nine comrades. The remaining soldiers form the new unit and return to duty. Necromancers raise the corpses of the killed soldiers and add them to the ranks of the Decimated, a small army of undead soldiers that drill eternally in the wastes southeast of Velecia Nora. The Decimated brigade is led by the graveknight General Fyodor Yelizaveta.
1017212491:
Spiro Spero

The ancient ruined fortress of Spiro Spero lies north of the Ndele Gap, on the Mwangi Expanse side of the Shattered Range. It takes its name from the spiral pathway that winds its way up the mountainside to its gate.
1020929511:
Scrapwall

Scrapwall is a town in the Sellen Hills built out of a gigantic technological junkyard almost 3 miles in length. Having never had a formal leader in its thousands of years of history, the city is instead run by disparate rival gangs.
1026623851:
Godsblood Crevasse

The Godsblood Crevasse is a large smoking crevasse that splits the earth of the Weeping Fields in the far south-east of the shadowy nation of Nidal, it also provides it with some of its finest gems. These gems are known as pigeon's-blood rubies and are believed to be a sacred gift from the nation's unholy divine patron Zon-Kuthon. Because of this, these gems are the only coloured gem considered fashionable by the Nidalese aristocracy. Getting these pigeon's-blood rubies is very difficult, as the smoke that escapes the Godsblood Crevasse is crimson in colour and is both incredibly caustic and poisonous, killing the living within minutes. Consequently, the Nidalese use petrified undead skeletons to mine out the rubies, overseen by the sadistic graveknight Grenda of Elith Lorin, a former member of the Midnight Guard and current member of the Umbral Court. These skeletons are generally voluntarily bequeathed by followers of Zon-Kuthon but the caustic smoke destroys them within a few months of working in the Godsblood Crevasse. Rumour has it Grenda is very unscrupulous about where she acquires her new skeletal labourers.
1031893071:
Black Whale

23 locations:
  • Aysepir's Astounded Abyss
  • Sea King Shipyards
  • Beneath the Docks
  • Brine
  • Barque Bazaar
  • Goblin King's Court
  • Little Inner Sea
  • Razorhall
  • Siphons
  • and 14 others…
1033037781:
Shark

The Shark is one of the ten remaining pilings of the Irespan in Magnimar. Standing 120 feet high, this piling is rumored to be haunted. There is an entrance in the upper piling.
1066914221:
Canalsguard

Canalsguard is a castle located next to Pastorling at the conjunction of the Lingian Prefecture's three largest canals. It was originally built to protect Taldor's canals from being used by any invading force from Qadira.

Canalsguard was originally built as a inspection and toll station, but during the Grand Campaign it was greatly expanded and heavily fortified. Since the end of the war with Qadira, Canalsguard has lingered as a hopelessly overbuilt relic of the war.

As of 4717 AR, it was guarded by a skeleton garrison barely large enough to manage the toll station. Most of the fortress is unused, except as a dumping ground by the Taldan military for washouts and screw-ups. Canalsguard's commander as of 4717 AR was Urbicus Porphygo, scion of middling House Porphygo in Oppara, and even with by Canalsguard's low standards he has not been a stellar commander of the fortress.
1070727681:
Wandering Monster

3 locations:
  • Bloom Cabaret
  • Chamber Grove
  • Dacilane Manor
1073453001:
Chennipon

Chennipon is a settlement in the distant nation of Vudra in Casmaron briefly mentioned in Paths Across Vudra by the author Liteim Krinas. The shipbuilders that live here are rumored to know the secrets of building flying ships.
1082462181:
Lightning's Call

Lightning's Call, also known as Lightning Top, is a mountain of the Five Kings Range located at the border of the Five Kings Mountains and Kyonin in central Avistan. It bears one of the Carved Kings sculptures featuring the visage of King Harral, which overlooks the southern side of the Great Pass.

The mountain derives its moniker from the perpetual gathering of storm clouds around its peak, a weather phenomenon that wizards of Tar-Kazmukh have determined is not magical in nature but remain mystified by.
1083609401:
Anandari Block

The Anandari Block is a Westgate neighborhood populated mostly by people of vudrani descent, and the architecture and food offered in the area reflect this style. The House Anandari are said to have been companions of Khiben-Sald in the past, and in just three generations, they became a prominent family and now control the entire neighborhood, which is patrolled by their Guild of Spears mercenaries.
1093918011:
Pidderack Shaft

The Pidderack Shaft is a mysterious vertical shaft found on the Nesmian Plains. Named for the family of farmers who discovered it when digging a new well, there is no established depth to the shaft, and the worked stone that lines the shaft gives no hints to its history. On the winter solstice, unusually large ghostly spiders emerge from the shaft. The farmhouse that surrounds the shaft has long been abandoned.
1095813171:
Skjoldmur

Skjoldmur is a small whaling town in the south of the island of Aegos, the larger island comprising Broken Bay in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings.
1104458931:
Vault of Tears

The Ustalavic county of Barstoi boasts three Pharasmin monasteries: the Chapel of Guilts; the Mother of Skulls; and the Vault of Tears. The Vault of Tears lies in the far north-east of the county between the Babelwood and the West Sellen River near the border of Ustalav with Numeria.
1115441381:
Adhaarm

Adhaarm is a small village that surrounds a monastery on the south-western edge of the Narhari Desert in Vudra. Its sole purpose is to feed the never-ending hunger of the great devourer Rudrakavala who dwells nearby.
1122933661:
Marstol

Marstol is one of the larger farming settlements in the Numerian Plains of Numeria.
1131415961:
Crossfen

Crossfen is a Nirmathi village in the Fangwood near the Lastwall border and a mile-wide wetland. Of the settlements inside the Fangwood, Crossfen is one of the few capable of supporting cattle that feed on thick-growing sedge.

The wetlands host boggards and will-o'-wisps known to claim visitors and villagers who attempt to cross it at night.
1133680471:
Absalom Mint

45 locations:
  • Archerus Manor
  • Forae Logos
  • Arcanamirium
  • Grand Council Hall
  • Blakros Museum
  • Primarch's Residence
  • White Grotto
  • Gutless Griffon
  • Embrey's Armory
  • and 36 others…
1141227861:
Tiantown

Tiantown is a neighborhood in the Foreign Quarter where most of the Tian Xia immigrants on Absalom reside. Although empires on that continent tend to be long-lasting, their fall results in great trauma for their inhabitants, who then move to Absalom and the most recent wave of migration came with the collapse of Lung Wa. Rival cultures and past enmities occasionally resurface, but the residents prefer the security of a familiar culture and language, blending with the more curious aspects of Absalom. Other unique residents include nagas, hobgoblins from Kaoling, and strange goblins never before seen in the Inner Sea.
1144992501:
Ecru

Ecru was a mining village in the Hollow Hills specializing in extracting valuable sulfur from nearby mines, which was sold at a good price to neighboring towns. The town fell under the control of the Ironfang Legion in 4717 AR and they used it as a base for transporting supplies throughout the region.
1151970561:
Downpour

Downpour is a small fishing town on Tempest Cay island in the Shackles. It was founded to take advantage of the plentiful fish that once made their way up and down the Bluehook River during spawning season. Since the cruel catoblepas Gruzugr made its home in the river, the waters have been poisoned and the fish are gone. The residents survive by harvesting the numerous nearby clam colonies.
1179072291:
Gurat

Gurat is a citadel-city nestled high in the Southern Zho Mountains. The imposing city clings to the edge of a precarious mountain spire and is home of the Mouthpiece of Gurat, an ancient cyclops who serves as oracle for the emperor of Kelesh. Visitors are not allowed near the Mouthpiece, who is guarded by a thousand deafened eunuchs. It is believed that the town is named after one of the Mouthpiece's ancestors.

Gurat is considered a holy place to most Qadirans, and pilgrims visit in search of enlightenment. The settlement itself is relatively small, resting on the smallest and flattest of the Zho Mountains, and is surrounded by an 8-foot wall. Gurati locals, known for their open-fire cooking and their weaving, often sit outside their homes, selling their wares to passersby.

The city also has scholarly colleges, with locals traveling there to engage in philosophical and mystical studies. Students are often seen in the streets, discussing such teachings with crowds and passer-byes. Aside from education and basic trade, specialty carpet weavings are often used for flying carpets and it is claimed that some of the woven patterns of their carpets hold secret prophesies, if one can find and interpret their own life thread path.
1184278091:
Pyramid of Kamaria

The Pyramid of Kamaria is the burial place for the Pharaoh Kamaria the Brazen, the only Osirian ruler to have openly worshiped the god Rovagug. It sits on the outskirts of the southern Osirian city of An, and dominates the settlement's southern skyline. The upper levels of the pyramid have long been cleared-out by tomb robbers, but are the home of a large cult of Rovagug; even they do not enter the lower trap-infested levels, however. The pyramid's central shaft dominates these dungeons, and it reaches all the way down to the Bloodcleft in the Darklands realm of Nar-Voth. A tribe of degenerate grimlocks is known to dwell near the shaft's base, and they occasionally trade strange artifacts and monstrous pets for slaves and food with the cultists above.

The first major task of pharaoh Gebessek IX, after the assassination of his despised mother, was to exterminate every Rovagug cultist in the pyramid, the entire population of the city of An, and seal the sublevels of the pyramid with potent magic and wards.

In the reign of pharaoh Remeses IV the cult of Rovagug sought to return to the pyramid. Remeses IV initially dealt harshly with the cult, now known as the Cult of Rovagug Made Visible and led by Eshemel Bhalloc, but later decided it was better to allow them to re-establish themselves within the pyramid rather than have them organise elsewhere in secret. This policy has been followed by subsequent pharaohs.
1186341941:
House of Lies

The House of Lies is the famous meeting place of the Guild of Liars, an organization of storytellers, con men, actors, and other conversational acrobats. Founded in 4354 AR and located on a river island in the part of Nidal's Uskwood north of the Usk River, the Guild meets here every five years to exchange stories, quips, tall tales, and other feats of verbal legerdemain. The person judged to be the most convincing liar is awarded the coveted title of "truth maker", and is given access to the House of Lies' extensive cabinet of curiosities.
1188718011:
Schloss Caromarc

Schloss Caromarc is an abandoned castle in the northeast of the Ustalavic county of Vieland, and is the former residence of Vieland's ex-ruler, Count Alpon Caromarc.
1210266831:
Wellspring Tower

The Wellspring Tower is one of the aeon towers that Aroden placed around the Isle of Kortos. The tower is a 300-foot-tall mudbrick step pyramid with a flat 10-foot-wide top and four uneven tiers.

The tower is located on the north side of the Isle of Kortos, near the Verdant Passage and the small town of Matten Cleave. It is known for having a sweet water spring at its base that provides water and greenery to the surrounding area.
1243084511:
Rabid Dog

The Rabid Dog is a tall, ramshackle tavern that sits amidst warehouses and mining offices east of the road through the Dawn Gate in Katapesh. Its owner is an old kholo named Moss Pelt who caters to kholo visitors and locals. The food served is distinctly "kholoish" in nature. The Rabid Dog is a dangerous place to frequent but it is said that a kholo named Shank Whitelock who spends much of his time in the Rabid Dog, is one of the greatest guides in the city. For a fee of 5 gp per day Shank can guide his clients to just about anywhere they want to go from Katapesh all the way to Solku.
1246503251:
Golden Aerie

2 locations:
  • Almas University
  • Cathedral of Aroden
1255027011:
Phaendar

The town of Phaendar is located in southern Nirmathas on the southern bank of the Marideth River near the Chernasardo region of the Fangwood. Despite its small size, it is one of the largest settlements in the Nesmian Plains. The town grew out of a Chelaxian stronghold which itself was built on top of an outpost of the Shining Crusade. The residents of Phaendar, most of whom are skilled craftspeople, mistrust authority and the town has little to no standing government. Most of the town's law enforcement needs are provided by the Chernasardo Rangers who frequent the town to trade for supplies.
1255668071:
Bogsbridge

5 locations:
  • Besmara's Temple
  • Blackwarn's Gallows
  • Cecaelia Swirl
  • Lilywhite
  • Queen Bes
1257515331:
Opparan Trade Commission

The Opparan Trade Commission is a guildhall established in 4714 AR for the purpose of carrying out commissions that benefit everyone, though a little more for Taldor wouldn't hurt. Merchants of the nation and nobles such as House Morilla, House Madinani, and House Tsoulet are especially associated with the commission.
1260949891:
Sanqatada Cinqarda

2 locations:
  • Shrine of Aroden
  • Vira Ciucci
1266406541:
Forest Elder

The Forest Elder is the oldest tree in all of Andoran and one of the oldest on the continent, with legends saying the entire forest grew up around it. In this clearing stands an enormous tree, larger than any city cathedral, with dark wood and roots that reach dozens of feet deep into the ground. Tatzlwyrms often nest in its branches, and members of the Greenfire Circle consider it a sacred place of pilgrimage.
1290621591:
Lair of Fafnheir

Dread Fafnheir is said to be the mightiest and oldest linnorm in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, and he has existed for as long as the Linnorm Kingdoms themselves. The Lair of Fafnheir is located deep in the Grungir Forest. A legend exists that whoever slays Fafnheir will become king of all the Ulfen, and unite the squabbling kingdoms into a single nation.
1294712621:
Maiaji

Maiaji (pronunciation MY-yee) was once the southernmost city in the nation of Hongal in Tian Xia. It is now the home to an army of ogres and hill giants, led by the ambitious ettin Nghiem.

Nghiem is known for his love of horse flesh, his short temper, and his unbelievable fecundity. At last count, Nghiem has more than 400 sons and has lost count of his daughters. While Nghiem and Maiaji theoretically swear allegiance to the oni rulers of Chu Ye, in reality Nghiem aspires to build a kingdom of his own and bows to no creature. The ruler of Hongal, Kiriltai Khan, is aware of the threat posed by Nghiem and Maiaji but has proved reluctant, at least so far, to send his own followers to deal with this threat, instead recruiting adventurers to try to deal with the problem.
1297725441:
Gold Manticore Fount

3 locations:
  • Atelier Kiritane
  • Moth and Flame
  • Shadeglass Foundry
1302660991:
Tomgruv

Tomgruv is located to the south of the Grungir Forest and to the north-east of Jol (the capital of Southmoor) in the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Today, it is officially ruled by Southmoor's Linnorm King Opir Eightfingers, but the local leaders will take control when they feel they can.

The town is on the road between Jol and the Kopparberget mine. It is built around the entrance to an old abandoned mine. The locals who are mainly farmers and herders use the mine as a stronghold and storage facility. A few miners work on meager copper veins in the surrounding hills.

Kobolds and other underground enemies dwell in the lower levels of the Tomgruv's abandoned mine but so far the residents have managed to keep the upper levels safe. Troll raiders find nothing worth outside the heavily fortified mine entrance.
1328226441:
Wati

Wati, also called the Half-City, is located in southern Osirion at the confluence of the Asp and Crook rivers and the birth of the River Sphinx. It is best-known for its enormous necropolis, or city of the dead, a relic of a bygone plague.
1330887851:
Brine

The Brine is an imposing stone prison house located in the Puddles district in Absalom. Lady Seichya of House Tevineg serves as the prison's head warden.

Ever since the regular flooding of the district, The Brine has ensured a slow but certain death for the criminals within. The holding pens is usually soaked with at least a foot of water from the flooding, but also raw sewage washes in from time to time, typically resulting in outbreaks of filth fever and general sickness.

Although the guards use elevated catwalks to stay out of the muck, the prisoners below frequently are forced to stand, sit and sleep in the foul waters.

Especially important individuals are sent to Black Whale prison.

The Salt Cartel fills its stocks of slaves from prisoners fraudulently collected from the Brine.
1331197861:
City of Thorns

The City of Thorns is a partially ruined and forgotten city in Kyonin hidden beneath a dome of tree-sized thorned brambles that was in turn obscured by powerful illusion magic.
1332739311:
Sandpoint

  • and 2 others…
1340565921:
Sihadrimon

Sihadrimon is one of the numerous prosperous coastal cities that dot the shores of the Betul Sea in the Western Ghats region of Vudra.
1345483581:
Marbledome

Marbledome is the central opera house of the Varisian city of Korvosa, and can be found in the upscale Heights District, just south of Castle Korvosa. Productions at the Marbledome, which is owned by House Jeggare, are always extremely lavish, but the operas performed there as of 4708 AR were often mediocre and rarely broke even. Nevertheless, it is one of the centers of Korvosan high society, with wealthy families clamoring for the best box seats.

As of 4708 AR, the Marbledome was (poorly) managed by Touran Palastus, a somewhat sadistic man who has driven more than one performer to relocate to Magnimar, or even the Sandpoint Theater.
1351577791:
Skull Hill

Skull Hill is a Chelaxian fort located in the southern reaches of the Hold of Belkzen in a region known as the Skittermounds.

The fortification marks the furthest extent of the Everwar campaign and the conquest of Cheliax. The fort was used for mercenary recruitment and weapons trading. Skull Hill consists of a motte and bailey keep, surrounded by a wooden wall with a surrounding moat.

Until recently, it was believed to have been destroyed not long after the events of the Everwar but, in fact, continued to be used as part of a spy network to monitor orc activities: an attempt by Cheliax to ensure the protection of its southern territories.

The fortress was invaded and sacked in 4709 AR by a tribe of orcs known as the Skittering Ravagers who had been terrorising the region for years.
1354311151:
Absalom Lighthouse

Absalom Lighthouse is located on Pilot Island at the center of Absalom's Flotsam Graveyard. It is a famed piece of architecture built under the command of the god Aroden. At 655 feet in height, the light from its tower can be seen from as far as 50 miles away on a clear night and at least 25 miles on stormy evenings or bright afternoons. All Inner Sea navigators know its light, which has helped make the City at the Center of the World a successful trade port. Along with the Blue Tower, the Spire of Nex, and the peaks of the Kortos Mounts, it is among the most recognizable parts of Absalom's skyline.
1356316971:
Mbaiki

In the past, the folk of the city of Mbaiki revered the animals of the jungle, and the jaguar above all others. The people all wished to emulate the jaguar—and something answered their call. The people rejoiced until it became clear that instead of being shapechangers they were condemned to live in jaguar form forever. In their rage they destroyed their city and then they moved into the jungle in small groups. It is said that some of the jungle's jaguars retain the knowledge of the Mbakai tribe, and can offer sage advice to those who know how to ask.

Today, the remains of the city are home to the most numerous tribe of halflings in the Kaava Lands, the Hazh'a. They revere Lamashtu and Zura, and collect the heads of intruders.
1357372981:
Crackspike

Crackspike is a formation of prospectors' bones in Devil's Perch, erected as both a warning and summer roost by the area's native Windspire tribe of strix around 4690 AR. The Chelaxian provincial authorities attempted to secure Crackspike around 4711 AR as a base for mining silver from Sorvus's Strike about two miles northwest of Crackspike; the strix subsequently burned the settlement to the ground.
1371107751:
Mosswater

The River Kingdoms settlement of Mosswater was established as a quiet fishing retreat by an exiled Ustalavic merchant named Artoor Geera. Geera's apothecary discovered a valuable process for making luminous dyes from the unusual glowing plant life of the nearby Glow Water Lake.

In 4664 AR a tribe of merrow moved into the area and slaughtered the inhabitants of the village. The aquatic ogres have driven off all subsequent attempts to re-occupy the site and take advantage of the lucrative dye-making process.
1373066081:
District Courthouse

The Westgate District Courthouse is a large, four-winged building where trials for the district are held and the prosecutor's offices are located. In the middle of the wings is a huge pool where, by tradition, suspects are brought to the edge to see their reflection in this natural mirror and clear their thoughts before entering court.
1384496161:
Evaren

The town of Evaren is a centre for trade between Iobaria's human and non-human populations. Located on the edge of the Syrzemyan Highlands, the town is still recovering from losing much of its population over the course of the past two plagues, though it does attract those tired of living solitary lives in the wilderness. The town is ruled by the Council of Three, the most notable being Councilor Pavla Trynar, regarded as the most powerful magic user in all of Iobaria. Trynar's many apprentices help to protect the town and keep the peace.
1391517431:
Razghora's Camp

A large number of displaced goblinoids gathered by the hobgoblin Razghora settled at Razghora's Camp in Druma.
1404231981:
Mount Branthlend

The tallest mountain in the Stolen Lands, Mount Branthlend rises from the landscape like a distinctively shaped monk's head. An incredible 5,400 feet tall, the mountain, also known as the Peak of Broken Promises, features a bare, knob-like pinnacle of white stone. The mountain grants its name to the range in which it is found: the Branthlend Mountains.

The mythos of the mountain is dominated by tales of Noarra, the great love of the barbarian lord Targran, who shouted curses from the peak of the mountain after the barbarian warlord took another woman as his queen. In that moment a great storm of fire and stone encompassed the peak of the mountain, and Noarra was said to be transformed into a great black dragon, who then rampaged across eastern Numeria. This myth attracts many travellers from throughout the land, drawn to the peak to cry out their own curses and oaths of vengeance.
1426743931:
Belila

Belila is a small Irriseni town of the Iceflow in the northwestern corner of the province of Bleakmarch. Much of the town recently converted to new faith known as the Way of Transcendent Bliss, much to the consternation of its ruler, the Baroness Frederyka.
1438360331:
Lyperro

Lyperro is a small village in the Rhuzam region of Druma.
1441170821:
Plumetown

4 locations:

Fort Inevitable

1455667721:
Court of Black Paper

The Court of Black Paper in Absalom is, in nearly equal parts, a powerful cartel-like law firm, a cutthroat school of legal studies, and a cult of Norgorber. Its ranking members are known as priest-advocates and are led by Senior Priest-Advocate Samel Maleagant, who has a reputation as the city's most prominent lawyer.
1458075451:
Moon's Eye

The Moon's Eye is a black granite formation located on the Nesmian Plains between the Deepcut River and the Marideth River. The formation has some religious significance to Kellid tribes in the area, dating back centuries. Explorers of the region occasionally report finding Kellid ruins in the area surrounding the Moon's Eye, though all reports are inconsistent of the location and description of these ruins. There are reports of unusually large wolves in the area as well.
1469683611:
Sea King Shipyards

4 locations:
  • Broadfoot Marshals Station House
  • Kortos Watercrafts
  • Margruel's Masts
  • Ogrekin Hall
1506324521:
Silverhall

Silverhall is a city that is located on the Awzera River in Brevoy, just before the river empties into Lake Reykal. This town is the family seat of House Lebeda, and it was here that the Lebeda family kept the Horn of Aroden for many years.
1512505371:
Mount Carissa

Mount Carissa is the seventh-tallest peak of the Five Kings Range at 15,126 feet. It is one of four mountains surrounding the city of Taggoret, alongside Mount Onik to its east, Mount Langley to its north, and Mount Soryu to its west.
1512593511:
Temple Row

2 locations:
  • Beneath the Docks
  • Black Whale
1517793071:
Plains of Conflict

The Plains of Conflict is a designated battleground in the Cradle region of the Impossible Kingdoms of Vudra. The mahajanapadas of the region boast swathes of incredibly fertile and valuable farmland, but many are also frequently at war with each other. To protect the farmlands from the effects of battle, the vast Plains of Conflicts were left unsettled and reserved for combat. The mahajanapadas of Kaurata and Pandata have been involved in intermittent hostilities there for centuries and shows no sign of abating.
1523221241:
Vigil's Hope

Vigil's Hope, located in the Precipice Quarter of Absalom, is the headquarters of the Knights of Lastwall and serves as an embassy for the exiled leadership of the destroyed nation of Lastwall. Watcher-Lord Ulthun II resides in Vigil's Hope, from where he coordinates the organization's diplomatic and fundraising activities.
1523559801:
Cindra's Exotic Pets

Cindra's Exotic Pets is a shop that promises to sell the most diverse animal species from the Inner Sea, including monkeys from the Mwangi Expanse, dinosaurs from Mediogalti Island, snakes from the Mana Wastes, crocodiles from Osirion, toads from the Sodden Lands, white ravens from Irrisen, wolves and foxes from Nirmathas, and much more.
1529699701:
Brinewall

Brinewall was an abandoned settlement and castle (see Brinewall Castle) built during the early part of the Chelaxian expansion into Varisia (see Everwar). Situated in northwestern Varisia where the Steam River flows into the Bunyip Bay, it was designed to encourage trade with, and fend off attacks from, raiders of the Lands of the Linnorm Kings. Recently, it gained a new lease of life as a port in the new kingdom of Eurythnia in New Thassilon.
1546133711:
Stoneshield House

Stoneshield House serves as the headquarters for the Graycloaks, the district watch of the Ascendant Court district of Absalom. Stoneshield House is named in honour of Dunarr Stoneshield, a former captain of the Graycloaks who is treated almost like a secular saint by serving members of the Graycloaks. The Graycloaks themselves are all atheists, allowing them to police the countless religions that call the Ascendant Court home with a sense of fairness, uninfluenced by any faith in the gods. They are currently led by Runewulf the Unbeliever, who lives within Stoneshield House. The building of Stoneshield House is understated in form, particularly when compared with the endless ostentatious temples and shrines within the district. Still, the Stoneshield House has its own imposing, ominous air to it. The Graycloaks are called on to deal with strange and powerful threats, and who knows what they have locked away within their headquarters.
1558657531:
Jaijarko Castle

Jaijarko Castle refers to the collection of slum dwellings in Magnimar's Rag's End section of the Beacon's Point district where the slumlord Rassimeri Jaijarko lives and runs his businesses. The various buildings that make up this complex are connected by secret passages.
1563141001:
New Vedagad

New Vedagad is the largest city in the storied Vudran region known as the Cradle.
1583813881:
Copperwood

Copperwood is a small town located northeast of Absalom just outside the city's walls that is the home of many of Absalom's laborers, tradesmen, and domestic servants. Originally a tent city of unskilled laborers who could not afford to live in the city proper, conditions steadily improved over the centuries until Copperwood became a town in its own right. The locals are often known as "Copperheads".
1590206551:
Temples of Pharaoh Ahn

The Temples of Pharaoh Ahn is a site in southeastern Osirion located between the Salt Hills and the Brazen Peaks from the brief reign of Pharaoh Ahn, who ruled after the Naga Pharaoh. Pharaoh Ahn believed himself to be as powerful as the mortal Nethys, to such a point that he was nearly driven insane. He ordered slaves and followers to this site in the Brazen Peaks in search of what his advisors told him might be the means used by Nethys to ascend to godhood.

While Pharaoh Ahn searched, his followers constructed inverted pyramids out of precious gemstones to serve as the first palace of what they believed would be their deity Ahn, and magically warded the structures from mortal incursions. Neither Pharaoh Ahn nor his followers returned, however, and later expeditions to the site found only bloody trails toward the pyramids.

As of 4714 AR, the site's only active researcher is Ali Ahmendi, who remained based in Sothis and claimed to be a descendant of Pharaoh Ahn's chief advisor. He employs adventurers in his attempts to explore the temples.

1622317951:
Hillhome

Hillhome is the surface name of an independent and reclusive underground settlement of kovintus located beneath the northeastern foothills of the Five Kings Mountains and northwestern Verduran Forest. Its true name is known only to its kovintus.
1642407691:
Courtaud

Courtaud is a settlement in the western corner of the Ustalavic county of Lozeri, near the edges of the southern Tusk Mountains. Rumors of a beast named the Devil in Gray have started to make the locals nervous, but the town's ruling council has yet to pay attention to them.
1645087701:
Imperial Marina

5 locations:
  • Cutlass Cove
  • Founder's Peak
  • Qatada Nessudidia
  • Wart Rock
  • Wave Door
1664894531:
Lake Platter

Lake Platter is the largest lake entirely inside Nirmathi borders. Cool all year long due to its mountainous sources, Lake Platter is five miles long but less than a mile wide. The lake boasts several fishing hamlets along its coast, and contains several bagiennik populations: amphibious newt-like fey with healing powers.
1669369151:
Habe's Sanatorium

Habe's Sanatorium, formally known as the Saintly Haven of Respite, is south of Sandpoint along Cougar Creek near the Ashen Rise.

Erin Habe used his wealth from years as a mental health and diseases expert in Magnimar to build the sanatorium. To raise funds for upkeep and his research, Habe had taken a silent partner, the elderly Varisian Caizarlu Zerren. Habe employs two cambion brothers, Gortus and Gurnak, as orderlies. Habe maintained a working laboratory where he conducted experiments on his patients that might cross ethical boundaries.

After Caizarlu was brought to justice by adventurers, Erin vowed to redeem himself and run his Sanatorium with the original purpose of helping those most in need.
1672866351:
Haunted Crater Lakes

The Haunted Crater Lakes are a series of five bodies of water northwest of the town of Slipcove on Bag Island. They are believed to have been created following impacts from meteorites several hundred years ago. The entire area is believed to be haunted by ghosts from the asteroid-belt known as the Diaspora according to Amberose Ysilde, who lives in Rumbutter.
1691686921:
Kora

The settlement of Kora, the seat of the Rampore Isles' administration in the Shackles, is surrounded by rice and sugar plantations. The crops are sold to the less self-sufficient islands in the Shackles, and the trained slaves who work these fields also serve as a commodity to be traded to slavers. Brutal overseers run the operations and mercilessly exploit the slave labor.
1692884011:
Aerduin's Folly

Aerduin was the capital of the Arch-Duchy of Melcat until the coming of the Living God, Razmir. When Duke Melcat refused to pledge his fealty to Razmir, the wizard destroyed the town and all of his inhabitants. Now known as Aerduin's Folly, the ruins have lain abandoned for 50 years.
1705745351:
Mount Sinatuk

Mount Sinatuk is a volcano at the far western end of the Five Kings Range, near the border of Druma and Isger. It is approximately two miles tall.

While the surrounding lands contain large fragments of black volcanic rock as evidence of an ancient eruption, as of 4712 AR the volcano had not been geographically active during the recorded history of the dwarves of the Five Kings Mountains. The nearby independent dwarven Regulate of Grimsburrow had used the dormant volcano's geothermic properties to provide heat, light, and steam power, and discovered mineable diamonds deep within its vacant lava tubes.
1708006661:
Corna Barracks

3 locations:
  • Vaneo Drovenge
  • Vira Grulios
  • Vira Oberigo
1727741551:
Ardis

Ardis is the former capital of the Immortal Principality of Ustalav. It is located in the eastern region of Soivoda, in the County of Ardeal and is the largest settlement in Ardeal. The city contains many decaying estates and fallow fields.
1748441991:
Pyre

The Pyre is a headland which extends into the Varisian Gulf just southwest of Sandpoint. Before Sandpoint was settled, Varisians used this area for rituals, including the Swallowtail Festival.
1750013171:
Gloaming Wood

The Gloaming Wood is a small and light evergreen woodland in the Nesmian Plains of Nirmathas, west of the Inkwater River. An elven ruin predating Earthfall known as Glemrielle is located inside the forest and contains many wards to drive out and control fey creatures that remain in effect; the Gloaming Wood still contains no fey creatures. A Pathfinder Society expedition led by the gnome Thisben Gibwane has been exploring the area, but the scholars are mainly gnomish and their fey nature has led them into deep paranoia.
1753125761:
Oregent

Oregent (pronounced OHR-eh-gehnt) is a city in central Andoran located south of Darkmoon Vale. It is home to the Lumber Consortium and acts as a gateway for traffic both to and from the Vale.
1761445061:
Waunomani

Waunomani (pronounced Wau-noh-MAA-nee), also known as Silungan, is the closest thing to a capital in the archipelago nation of Minata, off the eastern coast of Tian Xia. The diverse and heavily fortified city serves as a center of sea trade on the eastern coast of Belem and port for Minata's defensive fleet of warships.

"Waunomani" is a word of the Tau Nui people, a once-nomadic folk who claim descent from the ancient empire of Taumata and now primarily live in the Minatan isles of Rendah Pulu.
1764098251:
Dalaston

Dalaston is a large town on the southwestern edge of Taldor's World's Edge Mountains. Although members of all the major races reside in the town, dwarves are the second-largest group after humans. The three major employers are the local iron mine (owned by the Dalassenos family), the Morian Foundry, and the Irini brewery.

In 4709 AR, the red dragon Horranath attacked Dalaston.

In 4725 AR, the Godsrain awakened a dragon that devastated the village, and only a few inhabitants have not left the area.
1765524311:
Acropolis of the Thrallkeepers

The Acropolis of the Thrallkeepers is a ruined tower and dungeon located on a rise near the Wyvern Mountains.
1767918571:
Palace of Ivory and Bone

The Palace of Ivory and Bone is a perverse, desecrated fortress within the holy lands of the Divine Garden region of eastern Vudra.
1783059741:
Hyannis

Hyannis is an Ustalavic coal-mining settlement in the county of Versex; the forlorn shacks of the villagers are set on the edge of the Hungry Mountains near to the source of the Danver River. The village and the mines are still haunted by the greenish spectre of the witch called Black Aggie, whom the villagers rose up against nearly forty years ago; they ripped the monster to pieces then burnt her before casting her remains into an abandoned mine. Worse than the hauntings, though, are the miners who become possessed by what is considered Black Aggie's suffocating breath.
1788428421:
Westerhold

Westerhold is the richest of Absalom's outlying towns. Members of Absalom's wealthiest families own small estates in Westerhold. The town is also known for its large number of dwarven inhabitants and trustworthy stables.
1791639201:
Fort Indros
