1800364411:
Okim

Okim is one of the many cities of the Tian Xia nation of Linvarre, which was formerly the Taldan colony of Amanandar. Formerly a military outpost, Okim is now primarily known for its fireworks production, which is said to have drawn organized criminals from Seidoyaji and Todorokaze seeking to sell black powder across neighboring Kwanlai.
1808251701:
Jaha

Jaha is a great crumbling city in the northern part of the Mwangi Jungle in the Mwangi Expanse.
1841003871:
Chalk Harbor

The grim mining town of Chalk Harbor is the only functioning settlement on Whyrlis Rock island in the Shackles. The town is the base of operations of the slaver Alahandra Boisich, rumored to have once been a powerful baroness of Cheliax. The ruthless Alahandra captains the barkentine Cloudbuster's Dream.

There is no wildlife around Chalk Harbor save for bands of earth elementals and the few clusters of cullitox that lair underground, and no one can eat the sparse blighted crops that grow from the rocky soil. Water is drawn from the Elsewhere River, but all food is supplied by other islands and paid for by Alahandra.
1841557501:
Halls of the Closed Fist

The Halls of the Closed Fist, located in the northeastern third level of Urgir in the Hold of Belkzen, serves as the headquarters of the Closed Fist, an elite unit that enforces the will of the city's overlord. Under the rule of Ardax the White-Hair, the Halls also effectively serve as his seat of government, as he leaves the Great Palace vacant save for his predecessor Grask Uldeth's corpse.
1847953201:
Cauldrons of Calm and Calamity

The Cauldrons of Calm and Calamity are a series of mystical caves at the foot of Mount Keeba on the island of the Smoker in the Shackles. Within these caves, live the three prophets, known as Keeba's Eyes, who are famous in the Shackles for their wisdom and oracular prowess. Many pilgrims arrive in Plumetown seeking words of wisdom from the Eyes. They are informed by the residents there that they have to wait in the town until a Caretaker seeks them out, when one of the Eyes is willing to give an audience. The wait may last from hours to years. When a Caretaker does come to Plumetown with a summons, the silent figure escorts the petitioners on the long paved road to the Cauldrons of Calm and Calamity via the Wise Artery road. Upon arrival, they are guided to one of the Keeba's Eyes, who hears each petitioner's question and names the non-negotiable price for its answer.
1875081401:
Pastorling

Pastorling is a small Taldan hamlet that stands in both the literal and figurative shadow of the nearby fortress of Canalsguard. Pastorling is located at the intersection of three of the Lingian Prefecture's canals (alongside Canalsguard), and the reek of their stagnant waters has kept the hamlet free from significant aristocratic interference.

The Lingian Canals are notoriously dangerous, and at least some of this is due to one of Pastorling's residents: Lesah Voriovixis. To the town she is but a humble baker, but in reality she is the heart of a network of organised crime known as the Canal Syndicate. She cunningly encrypts her communications using a hidden cypher based on the braids and fillings of the pies she sends to certain underlings.
1881011561:
Ipeq

Located along the banks of the Crook River, Ipeq (pronounced ahy-PECK) is the third-largest city in southern Osirion. According to legend, Ipeq was conjured into existence in a single day by Pharaoh An-Hepsu VII. Ipeq stands on the southern bank of the river and completely blocks the Shallows of Ipeq, one of only three ways of crossing the Crook on foot.
1881957571:
Kalexcourt

Kalexcourt, the Fortress of Monoliths, or the Vault of the Witch-Priests, stands at the very north-westernmost point of Ustalav, in the county of Vieland, among the lower slopes of the Tusk Mountains. It is an ancient burial mound of the Kellid peoples dating from before the founding of Ustalav.
1892576311:
Snare-filled glade

The northernmost Narlmarches contain a snare-filled glade, trapped by a notorious trapper by the name of Breeg Orlivanch. While most trappers keep a close eye on their traps and feel a responsibility to warn others of their presence, Breeg finds cruel enjoyment in hiding his traps from animals and humans alike. Although the trapper is already dead, his specially crafted spike snares continue to litter the glade.
1895809771:
Oyster Cay

The port of Oyster Cay is in the Mainland region of the Shackles.

It is currently suffering as two Free Captains vie for control of the port, namely Havalas Grudd and his estranged lover Wide Olga.
1908938181:
Stavian's Hold

Stavian's Hold is a large town on the Whistling Plains, located on Taldor's eastern Porthmos Prefecture. The establishment of the settlement was motivated by Grand Prince Stavian III, who saw the project as a means to strengthen the power of the crown when he came to power, as well as divert the attention of his critics.
1910646761:
Xin-Gastash

Xin-Gastash was the capital of the Thassilonian Realm of Gluttony, and was destroyed by the great event that brought down the rest of the kingdom: Earthfall. Constructed underground so that the necromancers of Gastash could practice their arts without the detrimental interference of the sun, Xin-Gastash was said to have contained Thassilon's greatest bathhouses, drug dens, and feast halls. Though much of the city was destroyed and sealed off from outside contact by the earthquakes that shook Golarion following Earthfall, an obstructed entrance to Xin-Gastash exists beneath the mountain the orcs of Belkzen call the Sleeper.
1937138731:
Frog pond

One of the two foul-smelling hot springs at the source of the Skunk River also serves as a frog pond for two aggressive giant frogs. They fiercely defend their 150-foot-wide pond and its immediate surroundings, but are quick to retreat into the swampy, bubbling water when their life is threatened. For those who can tolerate the pungent smell of rotten eggs it releases, the warm waters of the pond are quite pleasant.

1961601191:
Gazbilah

Gazbilah is a Qadiran city located in the Northern Zho Mountains area, nestled in the foothills between two different spurs of the Zho Mountains. The Northern Zho Mountains are the poorest region in Qadira and face the neighbouring land of Taldor across the Jalrune River. As such, it is the least hungry for war with their neighbors.
1994165121:
Gruankus

The massive rune-covered wheel of Gruankus lies half-buried on the shore of the Varisian Gulf. Presumably a Thassilonian artifact, its original purpose remains unknown, much the same as the Cyphergate in Riddleport to its east. It was the location of the signing of the Treaty of Gruankus between the pirates of Riddleport and the leaders of Magnimar at some point after 4607 AR, ostensibly to reduce the danger of piracy to traders travelling the Varisian Gulf.

2012463781:
House of Ruin

The House of Ruin is a temple located on the border between Nex and Katapesh where jabali Surma dedicated her worship until she was defeated by followers of Sarenrae. In the past, a river flowed through the rocky valley, but without Surma's blessing, nature has vanished, and only the stone structure remains. In 4715 AR, some pathfinders ventured into the structure, defeating Surma, who had transformed into a div, and allowing Sinuhotep, who was trapped inside, to escape.
2029891691:
Dokeran

The ruined city of Dokeran, sometimes written Dokaren, stands on the Defaka River in the Mwangi Expanse. The city was destroyed several centuries ago by Tyruwat the Lost One using the artifact known as the Song of Extinction.

Tyruwat now lives beneath the city's grand temple, his life greatly extended by the years stolen from his cult and the local tribes using the Song. He is guarded by packs of howlers and a tribe of wild elves he has subjugated.

The people of Dokeran built the city of Saruhk nearly a thousand years ago as a place of pilgrimage for followers of Winlas, an empyreal lord, and Aroden.

A tapestry of the city hangs in Venture-Captain Nieford Sharrowsmith's office, which obscures the secret entrance to the Pathfinder lodge hidden within Sharrowsmith's Exports in Nantambu.
2030210171:
Vasterborg

Vasterborg is a heavily fortified town in the Irriseni province known as the Verge. It is located close to the border with the Lands of the Linnorm Kings, and a large part of its population consists of soldiers, ice trolls, and winter wolves.
2032399111:
Uiry Manor Museum

The Uiry Manor Museum was the residence of this noble family who held considerable influence in the city until 4673 AR. One night, a stranger arrived at the house, invited by the Scion Lord and when the butler went to another manor to get more wine, he returned to find a scene of carnage; everyone was dead, from the nobles to the children of the servants. The crime was never solved, and the family turned the house into a museum to avoid falling into poverty. Several guides explain the history of the artifacts and theories about how the murders might have occurred. Scion Lady Veridel was a schoolgirl when her family was murdered, and although she refuses to sleep in the manor, she also refuses to sell it for reasons that remain unclear.

2069595361:
Theumanexus College

The Theumanexus College is a small wizard school located in the East Shore district of the city of Korvosa.
2080054741:
Trilmsgitt Towers

The Trilmsgitt Towers in Molthune are three towers, each within several miles of the others. They were built by three brothers, each of whom was a wizard, and are believed to be connected to each other by magic. However, nothing has been heard from any of the brothers for some time, and the towers appear to have been abandoned for some unknown reason.
2082726231:
Hollow Garden

The Hollow Garden is a large entertainment module of the spaceship Divinity which was detached from its deck and slammed into Numeria, in the area now called Sovereign's Reach, during the Rain of Stars. It is currently run by Celmak Toysiel, a malfunctioning mannequin robot, who turned it into a trap for adventurers who have to provide 'entertainment' by traversing a dungeon customised by Celmak that only ends with their deaths.

Celmak uses the entertainment module's remaining functional sensors to scan for potential victims. When someone approaches within a mile of the Hollow Garden, it begins to emit pulses and holograms (particularly idyllic gardens) to entice them. The doors to the bunker are always open with flickering lights, outwardly resembling a recently opened, malfunctioning, undefended section of the Divinity, ripe for plunder. Once the prey enters, the door seals and the only escape from that point is death; no teleportation is possible within the Garden.

Celmak then greets explorers and explains that they are destined to make history; if they survive, then the rewards will be great, turning them into heroes rich beyond imagining. At the same time, it also scans their brains for their emotions and abilities, and analyses them to optimise the entertainment it provides, before opening the doors to the Hollow Garden. As its guests traverse the dungeon, fighting robots, and overcoming obstacles, Celmak records their thoughts and actions via hidden cameras, and crafts each encounter to test the responses of explorers, sometimes releasing psychoactive chemicals to 'heighten the drama', or offering commentary if its victims are having a too easy or too hard a time. Occasionally, a minor item from Celmak's hoard is given to its current victims, hand-picked to prolong the entertainment but never allowing success.

In addition to Celmak and its robots, the Hollow Garden has recently become refuge to a group of demons fleeing the Worldwound. They accepted Celmak's offer to be occasionally sent into scenes as desired, but their leader Vlathherex found that they cannot teleport within the Hollow Garden, and now suspects that Celmak will never allow them to leave, and seeks to disable this lock.
2083345651:
Armag's Tomb

Armag's Tomb is the resting place of the legendary Kellid hero Armag, and is believed to contain Ovinrbaane, the legendary Gorumite weapon believed to contain his entrapped soul.
2084817121:
Devil's Elbow Sea Cave

The Devil's Elbow Sea Cave was the home of the legendary siren Virashi, who arrived on the island over six centuries ago. After her death, it was inhabited by her spirit until the drow arrived and took over the cave to investigate the effects of the meteorite that had struck the island in 4708 AR.

2115408091:
Bildt

Bildt is one of the largest cities in the relatively sparsely populated Lands of the Linnorm Kings, and is located on the island of Aegos in the Steaming Sea. It is also the capital of the Linnorm Kingdom of Broken Bay.
2117688001:
Torcova

Torcova is a small town in northwestern Taldor in the southwestern foothills of the Fog Peaks in Northern Tandak Prefecture. Outside of the town, lies the manor home of a local noble, Lord Sauton Beane, who hides a terrible secret. He is secretly an ogrekin, albeit a handsome one, who has gone through seven wives in the hopes of begetting a human offspring. He has not succeeded so far, and has locked his monstrous progeny in a cave beneath his home after murdering their mothers. He only releases his ogrekin children to punish his enemies or kidnap his next wife.
2119994391:
Red Rune Canyon

Red Rune Canyon, located in the eastern Tusk Mountains of the Realm of the Mammoth Lords, is an interconnected maze of canyons tainted by the relentless expansion of the Worldwound. Festering black boils and sores weep red bloodlike mud from its walls. The canyons change form under the demonic influence, with blasphemous runes etched into Golarion's surface. The evil of Red Rune Canyon drives any living creature who enters it mad.

Larisa, a cambion sorcerer, arrived to Red Rune Canyon in 4713 AR. Using blasphemous sacrifices at each new moon, she gained control of the glyphs inscribed by the canyon. Each sacrifice calls forth a new symbol and siphons power from the distant Worldwound. When she bends the canyon into her grand final rune, she will get rid of her mortal blood and metamorphose into a full demon.

The reshaping of the Red Rune Canyon has weakened the barriers between Golarion and the Outer Rifts. At each of Larisa's rituals, a new gate is forced open for a few seconds, oozing demons out into the Tusk Mountains.
